from biomni.tool.database import blast_sequence, query_clinvar

# Step 1: define the sequence
sequence = ("")

print("Sequence length:", len(sequence))

print("\n1) Running BLAST (blastp) for the provided protein sequence...")
blast_result = blast_sequence(sequence=sequence, database="nr", program="blastp")
print("blast_result (raw) =")
print(blast_result)

# Extract top hit information if available
top_hit_title = None
if isinstance(blast_result, dict):
    top_hit_title = blast_result.get("title") or blast_result.get("hit_title") or blast_result.get("subject") or blast_result.get("top_hit")
print("\nTop BLAST hit title/identifier:", top_hit_title)

# Step 2: Query ClinVar for each candidate variant
variants = ["G418V", "P335A", "Y755C", "P777R"]
clinvar_results = {}

print("\n2) Querying ClinVar for candidate variants...")
for var in variants:
    prompt = f"ClinVar search for variant {var} in the protein with BLAST top hit: {top_hit_title}. Return clinical significance and review status."
    res = query_clinvar(prompt=prompt, max_results=10)
    clinvar_results[var] = res
    print(f"\nClinVar query for {var} ->")
    print(res)

print("\nCompleted ClinVar queries. Results saved in 'clinvar_results' variable.")
</execute>
</observation>
